description: Subject Knowledge Model - The knowledge model that every real expert carries in their head. This is the PRIMARY source for article generation, determining what a real expert would naturally explain.

gardening.name: Gardening
detectionRules: slug contains: garden, plant, soil, compost, mulch, fertilizer, pruning, harvest, vegetable, flower, herb, lawn, landscaping
title contains: garden, plant, soil, compost, mulch, fertilizer, pruning, harvest, vegetable, flower, herb, lawn, landscaping
requiredEntities: Plant Parts (Root, Stem, Leaf, Flower, Fruit)
Growing Media (Potting Mix, Garden Soil, Compost)
Sun Exposure (Full Sun, Partial Shade, Full Shade)
Plant Containers (Pots, Beds, Raised Beds, Containers)
mentalModels: The Soil Food Web - Understanding the ecosystem beneath the surface
Plant Stress Response - How plants react to environmental stressors
Seasonal Timing - Working with natural cycles rather than against them
Resource Allocation - Plants prioritize growth based on conditions
Succession Planting - Continuous harvests through staggered planting
Microclimate Management - Creating optimal conditions in small spaces
Integrated Pest Management - Balanced approach to pest control
Soil Building - Continuous improvement of soil health over time
typicalWorkflows: Site Assessment - Evaluate sunlight, soil, drainage, space
Soil Preparation - Test, amend, till, create beds
Plant Selection - Choose appropriate plants for zone and conditions
Planting - Seed starting, direct sowing, transplanting
Watering - Establish schedule, monitor moisture, adjust for weather
Feeding - Apply fertilizers based on plant needs and growth stage
Maintenance - Pruning, staking, weeding, pest monitoring
Harvesting - Pick at optimal maturity, handle properly
Seasonal Cleanup - Remove debris, protect plants, prepare for next season
requiredDecisions: Plant Selection - What to grow based on zone, space, and goals
Planting Timing - When to plant based on frost dates and season
Spacing - How far apart to plant based on mature size
Watering Schedule - Frequency and amount based on plant needs and weather
Fertilizer Choice - Type and application timing based on plant requirements
Pest Control Approach - Prevention vs. intervention methods
Harvest Timing - When to harvest for optimal quality and yield
Season Extension - Whether to extend growing season with protection
commonMistakes: Overwatering - More plants die from overwatering than underwatering
Planting Too Early - Not accounting for frost dates
Ignoring Soil Health - Focusing on plants without building soil
Overcrowding - Not allowing adequate space for mature plants
Wrong Plant Selection - Choosing plants unsuitable for zone or conditions
Inconsistent Watering - Alternating between drought and flood
Poor Sanitation - Not cleaning tools or removing diseased material
Ignoring Pests Until Damage is Severe - Missing early intervention window
Over-fertilizing - Burning plants with excess nutrients
Not Mulching - Losing moisture to evaporation and promoting weeds
tools: Hand Tools - Trowel, Pruners, Cultivator, Weeder
Power Tools - Tiller, Trimmer, Blower, Chainsaw
Measuring Tools - pH Meter, Moisture Meter, Rain Gauge
Watering Tools - Hose, Sprinkler, Drip Irrigation, Watering Can
Planting Tools - Bulb Planter, Seed Dispenser, Transplanter
Maintenance Tools - Pruning Saw, Loppers, Hedge Shears
Protection Tools - Row Covers, Netting, Fencing, Cloches
Harvest Tools - Basket, Shears, Knife, Gloves
measurements: N-P-K Ratios - Nitrogen, Phosphorus, Potassium percentages
USDA Zones - Hardiness zones based on minimum temperatures
Frost Dates - First and last average frost dates
Growing Degree Days - Heat accumulation for plant development
Moisture Levels - Soil water content percentages
Sun Hours - Daily sunlight duration requirements
Spacing Requirements - Distance between plants in inches/feet
Yield Measurements - Pounds per square foot or per plant
Germination Rates - Percentage of seeds that successfully sprout
processes: Composting Process - Decomposition of organic matter into humus
Seed Germination - Activation of seed growth under proper conditions
Photosynthesis - Conversion of light energy into chemical energy
Transpiration - Water movement through plants and evaporation
Nutrient Cycling - Breakdown and reuse of nutrients in ecosystem
Decomposition - Breakdown of organic matter by microorganisms
Pollination - Transfer of pollen between flowers for reproduction
Disease Progression - Infection, development, and spread of plant diseases
standards: Food Safety Standards for Home Gardens
bestPractices: Test soil before planting and amend based on results
Choose plants suited to your hardiness zone and growing conditions
Start with healthy soil - add organic matter annually
Water deeply and infrequently to encourage deep root growth
Mulch around plants to retain moisture and suppress weeds
Practice crop rotation to prevent nutrient depletion and disease
Monitor plants regularly for early signs of pests or problems
Clean tools between uses to prevent disease spread
Keep a garden journal to track what works and what doesn't
Plant in groups rather than single specimens for better pollination
frequentlyAskedQuestions: When should I start planting vegetables in my zone?
How often should I water my garden?
What's the difference between compost and fertilizer?
How do I know if my soil needs amendments?
What are the easiest vegetables for beginners to grow?
How can I control pests without using chemicals?
What's the best way to start seeds indoors?
How do I know when to harvest vegetables?
Can I grow vegetables in containers?
What should I do with my garden in the winter?

meal-planning.name: Meal Planning
detectionRules: slug contains: meal, plan, weekly, menu, prep, grocery, shopping, budget, nutrition, diet, cooking

requiredEntities: Food Groups (Vegetables, Fruits, Grains, Proteins, Dairy)

Energy Balance - Calories in vs. calories out
Nutrient Timing - When to eat different types of food
Habit Stacking - Building meal planning into existing routines
Batch Processing - Doing similar tasks together for efficiency
The 80/20 Rule - Consistency over perfection in eating habits
Seasonal Rhythm - Eating according to seasonal availability
Economy of Scale - Buying and cooking in larger quantities
typicalWorkflows: Weekly Planning - Review schedule, plan meals, create shopping list
Grocery Shopping - Shop with list, buy seasonal, check pantry first
Meal Prep - Batch cook proteins, chop vegetables, prepare grains
Daily Assembly - Assemble meals from prepped components
Inventory Management - Track what's used, what's expiring, what to buy
Budget Tracking - Monitor spending, adjust based on priorities
Recipe Selection - Choose based on time, ingredients, dietary needs
Cooking and Storage - Cook meals, portion, store properly
requiredDecisions: Meal Frequency - How many meals and snacks per day
Meal Composition - What foods to include in each meal
Cooking Method - How to prepare different foods
Portion Sizes - How much of each food to eat
Shopping Frequency - How often to buy groceries
Budget Allocation - How much to spend on different food categories
Prep Strategy - What to prep ahead vs. cook fresh
Dietary Restrictions - What foods to include or exclude
commonMistakes: Overcomplicating Meal Plans - Trying too many new recipes at once
Not Prepping in Advance - Cooking from scratch every day
Ignoring Portion Sizes - Underestimating or overestimating servings
Not Accounting for Leftovers - Cooking too much or too little
Skipping Planning - Deciding meals on the fly leads to poor choices
Not Checking Inventory - Buying duplicates or missing ingredients
Ignoring Seasonality - Buying out-of-season produce at high prices
Not Adapting Recipes - Not adjusting for dietary needs or preferences
Poor Storage - Not storing food properly leading to waste
Not Tracking Spending - Going over budget without realizing it
tools: Planning Tools - Meal planning apps, calendars, notebooks
Cooking Tools - Slow cooker, instant pot, sheet pans, food processor
Storage Tools - Glass containers, Mason jars, freezer bags, vacuum sealer
Measuring Tools - Kitchen scale, measuring cups, measuring spoons
Prep Tools - Cutting boards, knives, mixing bowls, storage containers
Shopping Tools - Reusable bags, shopping list app, price comparison tools
Tracking Tools - Food diary, macro tracking app, budget tracker
measurements: Macronutrients - Grams of protein, carbs, fats
Micronutrients - Milligrams of vitamins and minerals
Portion Sizes - Cups, ounces, tablespoons, servings
Cost Per Meal - Total cost divided by number of servings
Time Per Meal - Preparation and cooking time
Shelf Life - Days until food spoils
Glycemic Index - Impact on blood sugar
Serving Size - Standardized portion for nutritional information
processes: Meal Planning Process - Assessment, selection, scheduling, shopping
Grocery Shopping Process - List making, shopping, storing
Meal Prep Process - Cooking, portioning, storing
Storage Process - Cooling, packaging, labeling, storing
bestPractices: Plan meals around what's on sale and seasonal
Batch cook proteins and grains at the start of the week
Keep a well-stocked pantry with versatile staples
Use a shopping list and stick to it
Store food properly to maximize shelf life
Repurpose leftovers into new meals
Track your grocery spending to stay on budget
Choose recipes with overlapping ingredients
Prep vegetables when you bring them home
Keep emergency meals for busy days

requiredDecisions: Recipe Selection - What to cook based on available ingredients and time

processes: Preparation Process - Washing, peeling, chopping, measuring

Flavor Development - Building layers of flavor

bestPractices: Read the entire recipe before starting
Prepare all ingredients before cooking (mise en place)
Use sharp knives for safety and precision
Measure ingredients accurately
Preheat pans and ovens before cooking
Season food throughout the cooking process
Let meat rest after cooking
Taste and adjust seasoning before serving
Clean as you go to maintain organization
Use proper cooking temperatures for food safety

cloud.name: Cloud Computing
detectionRules: slug contains: cloud, aws, azure, gcp, serverless, lambda, ec2, s3, kubernetes, docker, container, microservices, devops

mentalModels: Shared Responsibility Model - Security responsibilities between provider and customer
Pay-As-You-Go - Cost based on actual usage
Elasticity - Scaling resources up and down automatically
Immutable Infrastructure - Replace rather than modify infrastructure
Cattle vs Pets - Treating infrastructure as disposable resources
Defense in Depth - Multiple layers of security
Least Privilege - Minimum necessary access permissions
Event-Driven Architecture - Systems responding to events
typicalWorkflows: Infrastructure Provisioning - Define infrastructure, deploy using IaC
Application Deployment - Build, test, deploy applications
Scaling - Monitor metrics, adjust capacity automatically or manually
Monitoring and Alerting - Set up monitoring, configure alerts for issues
Backup and Recovery - Create backups, test restoration procedures
Security Hardening - Configure security groups, IAM policies, encryption
Cost Management - Monitor usage, optimize resources, set budgets
Disaster Recovery - Plan and implement recovery procedures
requiredDecisions: Cloud Provider Selection - AWS, Azure, GCP based on needs and expertise
Service Selection - Which services to use for specific requirements
Architecture Design - How to structure applications for the cloud
Scaling Strategy - Vertical vs. horizontal scaling approaches
Security Model - IAM policies, encryption, network security
Cost Optimization - Reserved instances, spot instances, right-sizing
High Availability Design - Multi-AZ, multi-region strategies
Disaster Recovery Approach - Backup strategies, failover procedures
tools: Security Tools (Security Hub, Inspector, Prisma Cloud)
Cost Management Tools (Cost Explorer, Trusted Advisor)
Development Tools (AWS CLI, Azure CLI, SDKs)
measurements: Application Metrics (Response time, Error rate, Throughput)
Cost Metrics (Monthly spend, cost per resource)
bestPractices: Use Infrastructure as Code for all deployments
Implement least privilege IAM policies
Use multi-AZ deployments for high availability
Implement proper monitoring and alerting
Regularly backup data and test restoration
Use auto scaling to handle traffic changes
Encrypt data at rest and in transit
Regularly review and optimize costs
Implement proper logging and auditing
Use managed services when possible
